channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: When gamification in classrooms distracts more than it teaches
item.description: The article discusses how gamification—using points, badges, leaderboards and other game‑like elements—is being brought into Indian classrooms to make learning more engaging. It explains that while th...

item.title: CRTC mandates online self-service for telecom plan changes and cancellations
item.description: The Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission (CRTC) has directed all cell phone and internet service providers to give customers the option to modify or cancel their subscription se...

item.title: Victim in Mugabe shooting case receives partial compensation as missing firearm remains a concern
item.description: A worker who was shot during an incident at the Mugabe family home in Hyde Park, Johannesburg, has received R250,000 in compensation, with a further R150,000 still expected. This detail emerged during...

item.title: Senate Antisemitism Report Omits Islamic Extremism Despite Foiled Plots
item.description: A recent Senate of Canada report on the unprecedented rise in antisemitism fails to mention Islamic extremism as a factor, despite numerous foiled terror plots targeting Jews during the study period. ...

item.title: Obando Residents Face Health and Environmental Risks After Navotas Landfill Fire
item.description: A recent fire at the Navotas landfill has caused concern among residents of Obando, Bulacan. While the incident occurred outside their municipality, the smoke and fumes from the burning waste have aff...

item.title: USAf addresses concerns over challenges in South Africa's universities
item.description: Universities South Africa (USAf) has responded critically to an article published by Daily Investor claiming that South Africa's largest universities are collapsing. Dr Phethiwe Matutu, CEO of USAf, e...

item.title: Australia ramps up diplomacy as Middle East tensions threaten fuel supply and economy
item.description: Australia’s Foreign Minister Penny Wong has undertaken a rapid series of high-level diplomatic talks in response to escalating tensions in the Middle East and their potential economic fallout. Her dis...

item.title: Duterte Legal Team to File Appeal Against ICC Charge Confirmation
item.description: Rodrigo Duterte’s legal team is preparing to file an appeal against the International Criminal Court (ICC) Pre-Trial Chamber I's confirmation of charges relating to killings during his war on drugs, b...

item.title: Nakuru Residents Confront Man Over Alleged Ritual Activities in Airbnb
item.description: In Nakuru county, specifically in the Naka estate, residents were shocked after a man renting short-stay apartments was allegedly caught performing unusual rituals. Neighbours reported strange behavio...

item.title: UK affirms unwavering stance on Falklands following leaked Pentagon memo
item.description: Downing Street has reaffirmed that the United Kingdom's position on the Falkland Islands remains resolute and unchanged, following the leak of an internal Pentagon memo. The email reportedly suggested...

item.title: Highlights and Results from the 2026 Fortuner Challenge in the Cape Winelands
item.description: The 2026 Fortuner Challenge, held in the picturesque Cape Winelands, was an action-packed two-day event where four teams competed for a brand-new Toyota Fortuner. The competition saw teams of media re...

item.title: Growing support for higher taxes on Australia’s gas exports amid Senate inquiry
item.description: Pressure is mounting in Australia for reforms to the way gas exports are taxed, with a Senate inquiry hearing strong criticism of the current system. Former Treasury secretary Ken Henry told the inqui...

item.title: Activist Charged Over Alleged Extortion Attempt Against KWS Director-General
item.description: A 40-year-old activist, Francis Awino Onyango, has been charged in a Nairobi court for attempting to extort Ksh1.7 million from the Director-General of Kenya Wildlife Service (KWS), Erastus Kanga. Ony...

item.title: Alex Eala exits Madrid Open in second round for third consecutive year
item.description: Alex Eala's pursuit of a third-round appearance at the Madrid Open remains elusive after she was defeated by world No. 21 Elise Mertens of Belgium in the second round. The Filipina tennis star suffere...

item.title: Melbourne father protects family from armed intruders using ironing board
item.description: In Ivanhoe East, Melbourne, a father became a local hero after confronting four teenagers armed with machetes during a home invasion. The incident occurred early Thursday morning when his wife alerted...

item.title: Lewis Moody discusses MND diagnosis, family impact and determination to support research
item.description: Former England rugby captain Lewis Moody has spoken candidly about his diagnosis with motor neurone disease (MND) in a conversation with his former coach Sir Clive Woodward. Moody, now 47, revealed he...

item.title: Zoe Kravitz reportedly engaged to Harry Styles following previous relationships
item.description: Zoe Kravitz, 37, appears to be engaged to singer Harry Styles, 32, after eight months of dating. This would mark her first engagement since splitting from ex-husband Karl Glusman and ex-fiancé Channin...

item.title: Profile of Prominent Restaurant Owners in Victoria
item.description: This article highlights the significant figures in Victoria's restaurant industry, focusing on influential business owners who have shaped the local culinary landscape. It discusses their business ven...

item.title: Federal High Court rules police and FRSC cannot fine motorists without court order
item.description: Na Abuja, Federal High Court don issue judgment wey stop Nigeria Police Force (NPF) and Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC) from dey fine motorists for third-party motor vehicle insurance matters. Judge ...

item.title: Mitchells Plain primary school responds to alleged sexual assault incident
item.description: Parents at Seaview Primary School in Rocklands, Mitchells Plain, have expressed concern following reports that a Grade 5 learner allegedly attempted a sexual assault on another learner in the school t...
